message:
ruqu-backend v0.1 — Stabilizer backend (Aaronson-Gottesman tableau)

Polynomial-time Clifford-only simulation alongside the v0.0
StateVector path. New `RuquStabilizerBackend` mirrors the existing
adapter shape — operators construct, `execute(name, circuit)`, and
register with `RuLake`. The simulator is the canonical 2n+1 row
tableau (n destabilizers, n stabilizers, scratch row), bit-packed
into Vec<u64>; gate updates for H, S, X, Y, Z, CX run in O(n) per
gate. Non-Clifford gates (T, Rz) are refused with a descriptive
StabilizerError carrying the gate name.

Witness flow keeps the per-backend tag — same circuit on the two
backends produces distinct witnesses in v0.1; the concordance-mode
strip per ADR-008 §G4 ships in v0.2 (test marker added, #[ignore]).

Verification:
  - cargo build --release: zero warnings
  - cargo test  --release: 24 passed, 1 ignored
                            (10 lib + 8 stabilizer unit + 3 sv smoke
                             + 3 stab smoke; concordance test ignored)
  - cargo bench --bench stabilizer -- --quick:
      * stabilizer n=14: 2.66 µs   (state_vector n=14: 815 µs → ~308×)
      * stabilizer n=16: 3.37 µs   (~20 G tableau-cells/s)
      * stabilizer n=24: 7.59 µs
      * stabilizer n=32: 16.4 µs   (state_vector n=32: not tractable)

No new transitive deps — bit-packing is hand-rolled over Vec<u64>.
The v0.0 StateVector backend is untouched.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -1,34 +1,40 @@
 # ruQu backend — quantum execution BackendAdapter (ADR-008).
 #
-# v0.0 ships the StateVector backend only — small-N exact simulation
-# with witness-anchored execution that round-trips through the ruLake
-# cache. Stabilizer / Clifford+T / TensorNetwork / Hardware backends
-# land in v0.1 / v0.2 per ADR-008 §4 and the ship table in
+# v0.0 shipped the StateVector backend (small-N exact simulation with
+# witness-anchored execution that round-trips through the ruLake
+# cache). v0.1 adds the Stabilizer backend (Aaronson–Gottesman
+# tableau, polynomial-time Clifford-only simulation, qubit cap 32).
+# Clifford+T / TensorNetwork / Hardware backends land in v0.2 per
+# ADR-008 §4 and the ship table in
 # docs/research/ruqu/integration-with-rulake.md §1.6.
 #
 # Standalone Cargo package per ADR-001 (no root workspace; rulake from
 # crates.io v2.2 is the only ruLake-side dep).
 #
-# Scope of v0.0:
-#   - `RuquStateVectorBackend` (≤16 qubits to keep RAM bounded;
-#     production uses up to 25).
-#   - `Circuit` mini-IR: H, X, Y, Z, S, T, CNOT, RZ.
+# Scope of v0.1:
+#   - `RuquStateVectorBackend` (≤16 qubits, exact f64 amplitudes).
+#   - `RuquStabilizerBackend` (≤32 qubits, Clifford-only via tableau).
+#   - `Circuit` mini-IR: H, X, Y, Z, S, T, CNOT, RZ. Stabilizer
+#     refuses T and Rz with a descriptive `StabilizerError`.
 #   - Witness derivation byte-isomorphic to a `RuLakeBundle` —
-#     `RuLakeBundle::new(data_ref="ruqu://<circuit-id>", dim=Hilbert,
-#     rotation_seed, rerank_factor=1, generation)`.
+#     `RuLakeBundle::new(data_ref="ruqu://<circuit-id>@<backend-tag>",
+#     dim, rotation_seed, rerank_factor=1, generation)`. The backend
+#     tag (`"sv"` vs `"stabilizer"`) flows into the witness; the
+#     concordance-mode strip per ADR-008 §G4 lands in v0.2.
 #
 # Build:  cargo build --release
 # Test:   cargo test --release
+# Bench:  cargo bench --bench stabilizer
 
 [package]
 name        = "ruvector-rulake-ruqu"
-version     = "0.0.1"
+version     = "0.1.0"
 edition     = "2021"
 rust-version = "1.89"
 license     = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
 authors     = ["Ruvector Team"]
 repository  = "https://github.com/ruvnet/RuLake"
-description = "ruQu v2 BackendAdapter for ruLake — quantum execution substrate, StateVector backend (ADR-008)"
+description = "ruQu v2 BackendAdapter for ruLake — quantum execution substrate, StateVector + Stabilizer backends (ADR-008)"
 readme      = "README.md"
 
 [dependencies]
@@ -51,6 +57,10 @@ harness = false
 name    = "execute_and_pull"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name    = "stabilizer"
+harness = false
+
 [profile.release]
 opt-level     = 3
 lto           = "thin"
